spec_match.rationale: Each scenario uses the correct stripped/kept fields. The cookie-only/header/join-keys/server-side dedup framing reflects how ad-tech pipelines actually use debug keys.

interactive.rationale: Four scenario buttons swap the before/after panes, the checklist, and the team summary — distinct interactive surfaces per scenario.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises pick-your-setup + tailored diff + checklist + team message — page delivers all four.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers four common pipelines but doesn't mark the silent-dedup-collision risk as severe; given the chromestatus motivation calls this out as a 'pipeline-breaking' failure mode, the visual treatment is too neutral.

references.rationale: See-also section links to siblings and the spec root but NOT the chromestatus.com/feature/6257907243679744 link. Rule #3.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, scenario-btn aria-pressed override sets background AND color together, log-pane stripped/kept use rose/emerald correctly.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: References only point to spec root and sibling concepts. Add https://chromestatus.com/feature/6257907243679744 per rule #3.

openQuestions.title: Highlight the server-side dedup-collision scenario
openQuestions.detail: The server-side dedup scenario is the actual pipeline-breaking case: when debug keys disappear, dedup keys collide and conversion counts double. Promote that scenario visually (e.g. a 'breaks pipelines' badge) so teams using that pattern can find it immediately.

openQuestions.title: Add a download-checklist button
openQuestions.detail: Once a scenario is picked, the visitor likely wants to share the checklist with their team. Add a 'copy as Markdown' button that serialises the visible checklist + summary so it can be pasted into a doc or PR.
